w:r.w:t: Code Review | Issue Analysis | Fix Plan | Agent Prompt
Before listing issues, it is important to acknowledge what is working correctly and well-implemented. These areas should NOT be changed.
Vite + React setup with vite-plugin-pwa correctly configured — PWA works.
All 11 LocalStorage collection keys match the SRS specification exactly.
storage.js — all 6 functions (saveData, getData, getConfig, addRecord, updateRecord, addPayment) are correctly implemented.
calculations.js — all 9 functions are correctly implemented with proper logic.
billId.js — HB-SSS-YYYY-NNN format and cancelled-job exclusion rule are correctly implemented.
timeUtils.js — getDuration() with overnight guard is correctly implemented.
idGenerator.js — auto-ID generation with all prefixes is correct.
initialData.js — F000 Own Farm reserved record and default pricing config are correct.
PricingConfig.jsx — reads/writes correctly, all 7 rate fields present, saves with lastUpdated.
Farmers.jsx — CRUD works, F000 delete/edit guard is present.
Drivers.jsx — CRUD works, salaryRatePerHour field correctly saved.
DriverEntry.jsx — sessions UI, overnight guard, diesel modes (all 3), and pricePerLitre auto-fill from config all work correctly.
Harvester.jsx — Bill ID generation, log-linking with already-linked guard, dieselFromLogs auto-sum, payments[] array pattern, PDF export all work correctly.
Rental.jsx — payments[] array, machine unit auto-detection, rate from config, PDF export all work correctly.
PaymentHistory.jsx — fully correct reusable component with payment modes (cash/bank/UPI).
LogLinker.jsx — correctly shows only unlinked logs, filtered by farmer+machine.
machineTypes.js, seasons.js, workTypes.js — all data files are correctly structured.
What is wrong: App.jsx goes directly to Dashboard. No login screen exists. Anyone opening the app has full admin access to all data. There are no role-based routing guards.
Why it is a problem: The SRS defines 3 roles with different access rights:
Admin — full access | Driver — log entry + own salary view | Farmer — own records only
Without authentication, a farmer can access pricing config, all other farmers' data,
and every financial record. A driver can edit pricing. Nothing is protected.
What was expected: A Login.jsx page with role selection and password/PIN entry.
Role stored in localStorage as session. Every route guarded by role check in App.jsx.
What is wrong: No FarmerView.jsx page exists. No route exists for farmers to see their own data.
There is no way for a farmer to view bills, Bill IDs, payment history, or balance.
Why it is a problem: Farmer View is one of the 3 core user roles (WHO) in the documentation.
It is the farmer's primary touchpoint with the system. Without it, the system
cannot replace the current manual billing process for farmer communication.
What was expected: A page where farmer selects their name, then sees:
— All Harvester Bills with Bill IDs and payment status
— All Rental charges with payment history
— Total outstanding balance across all bills
Finance Ledger Module (Lending + Home Expenses) Missing
What is wrong: Expenses.jsx exists but only tracks general business expenses.
There is NO lending/repayment tracking module at all.
The lendingRecords collection (rl_lending) is never read or written anywhere in the codebase.
Why it is a problem: Informal lending is a core real-world business activity
for the client. The SRS defines a full Finance Ledger module with:
— Lending: person name, amount given, repayments[], outstanding balance, status
— Home Expense: separate from business expenses, with mealTime for food entries
What was expected: A Finance.jsx page with two sub-sections.
Lending records must have repayments[] array (same pattern as payments[]).
What is wrong: Reports.jsx does not exist. There is no reporting capability at all.
Why it is a problem: Reports are how the admin answers the most important daily questions:
'What did I earn this month?' | 'Which farmers still owe money?'
'What do I owe each driver?' | 'How profitable was the Kuruvai season?'
Without reports, the system does not fulfil its primary purpose of replacing notebooks.
What was expected: Reports.jsx with 5 report types from the SRS:
1. Monthly Business Summary (income / expense / profit by month)
2. Machine-wise Report (tyre vs track revenue / profit)
3. Farmer Balance Report (who owes how much)
4. Driver Salary Report (hours / earned / paid / balance per driver)
5. Harvester Season Report (all jobs in KUR / SAM / THA season)
The dashboard totals ALL records ever created, not just the current month.
Income and expense figures grow forever and are not actionable.
SRS requires a month selector with default = current month.
ERROR B — Harvester Job Expenses Not Counted:
The dashboard sums harvester finalAmount as income, but never subtracts the job-level
expenses (dieselFromLogs, additionalDiesel, otherExpenses). These are inside each job
record but the dashboard ignores them entirely. Profit is overstated.
ERROR C — Wrong Data Key for Worker Salary:
Dashboard reads from 'rl_worker_salary' (Workers.jsx salary log, a custom key).
This is an ad-hoc key not in the SRS schema. The SRS uses rl_work_entries for
work records with netPayable, not a separate salary payment key.
ERROR D — No Pending Farmer Balance or Driver Salary Unpaid Displays:
SRS requires dashboard to show: pending harvester bill count, total outstanding
farmer balance, and total unpaid driver salary. None of these exist.
Worker Module Uses Wrong Formula — SRS Logic Not Implemented
What is wrong: Workers.jsx has two separate forms (Work Entry + Salary Payment) that
do not match the SRS schema at all. The net payable formula is completely absent:
Current code saves 'Daily Work Entry' with just {date, workerId, workType, amount}
and separately saves 'Salary Payment' with {workerId, amount, notes} to rl_worker_salary.
1. The advance / bonus / extraAmount fields are completely missing.
2. The netPayable formula is never computed.
3. The worker salary is saved to 'rl_worker_salary' (a non-SRS key),
but work entries go to 'rl_work_entries'. Dashboard reads from rl_worker_salary
What was expected: Single work entry form with baseSalary, advance, bonus,
extraAmount fields. netPayable computed live. Warning if negative.
Driver Salary Module Logic Is Fundamentally Broken
What is wrong: SalaryDriver.jsx calculates salary by summing ALL logs for a driver
across all time — not just unpaid hours for the current pay period.
Problem 1 — Cumulative overcounting: If a driver worked 100 hours total and has been
paid 3 times, each new salary record will show 100 hours of base salary instead of
the new hours since last payment. The salary grows exponentially and incorrectly.
Problem 2 — No payments[] array pattern: SRS requires partial payment tracking
with addPayment(). The current implementation has a single 'Confirm Payment' button
that creates one final record — no partial payments, no balance tracking.
Problem 3 — Developer's own comment reveals awareness of the bug:
'// Wait, my DriverEntry doesn't have a Driver ID select yet?'
(It actually does — the comment is stale but the concern about logic was valid.)
What was expected: System detects days with logs that have no salary entry yet.
Creates per-day salary entries. Admin records partial payments over time.
Running balance tracks unpaid amount per driver.
Harvester Job Missing otherExpenses UI — Net Profit Wrong
What is wrong: The Harvester form initialises otherExpenses: [] but has no UI
to add entries (worker salary, mechanic costs, spare parts, driver food).
The totalExpense calculation only adds dieselFromLogs + additionalDiesel:
Missing from calculation: sum of otherExpenses[] items.
Result: netProfit is overstated for every harvester job that has non-diesel costs.
Fix required: Add a dynamic 'Add Expense' row system (category dropdown + amount)
and include sum(otherExpenses) in the totalExpense calculation.
No Input Validation or Inline Error Messages
What is wrong: DriverEntry.jsx only validates driverId with an alert().
All other validations are absent. The SRS defines these required validations:
1. End time must be after start time — show inline error per session row
2. Diesel value must be > 0 when mode is not 'none'
3. All required fields must show red border + Tamil error message on submit
Using alert() dialogs (also in Farmers.jsx delete) violates the SRS UX requirement
for inline Tamil error messages. alert() is English-only and disruptive on mobile.
Fix required: Add validation logic before save in each module. Show errors inline.
Replace window.confirm() with a proper ConfirmDialog component.
Expenses.jsx Has No source Field — Dashboard Breaks
What is wrong: Expenses.jsx saves records without a 'source' field:
{ id, date, category, amount, description } — source is MISSING
The SRS mandates source: 'own_farm' | 'home_expense' on every expense record.
Without it, the Dashboard cannot segregate business from home expenses for profit.
General expenses (machine maintenance, fuel) will be mixed with home groceries.
Additionally, Expenses.jsx is meant to be the 'home expense + general expense'
tracker but currently has no way to tag expenses as 'own_farm' vs 'home_expense'.
Fix required: Add source field (enum dropdown) to Expenses.jsx form.
Dashboard must filter expenses by source when computing business profit.
What is wrong: OwnFarmIncome.jsx uses this schema:
SRS requires this schema for rl_own_farm_income:
Where totalIncome = numberOfBags x pricePerBag (computed, never entered manually)
— No cropType field (uses a different dropdown)
— Amount entered directly as a lump sum (not computed from bags x price)
— Source categories (subsidy, straw_sale) are not in the SRS
Fix required: Replace form fields to match SRS schema exactly.
What is wrong: No JSON export or import functionality exists anywhere in the codebase.
The lendingRecords, ownFarmIncome, and all 11 collections have no backup path.
Why it is a problem: LocalStorage is device-bound. If the user clears browser data,
Chrome is updated, or device changes, ALL data is permanently lost.
For a real business replacing paper notebooks, this is an unacceptable risk.
— 'Export Data' button downloads JSON file with schemaVersion: '2.0.0'
— 'Restore Data' file picker validates schemaVersion before importing
— Wrong version shows Tamil error message and blocks restore
What is wrong: Farmers.jsx form default type is 'external' and offers only
'external' and 'own' as options. SRS schema defines:
'external' does not exist in the SRS data model.
All farmers added through this form will have type='external', which no other
module understands or filters on. The 'neighbour' and 'normal' farmer types
are never usable since they're not in the dropdown.
Fix: Change dropdown options to: own | neighbour | normal with Tamil labels.
App.jsx Seeding Uses getData() Instead of getConfig()
getData() returns [] (empty array) by default for non-existent keys.
Object.keys([]) returns [] with length 0, so the condition accidentally works.
But it is semantically wrong — getData is for arrays, getConfig is for objects.
This is a latent bug — if getData behavior changes or is refactored,
What is wrong: The Dashboard shows ALL-TIME totals with no date filter.
As data accumulates over weeks and months, the numbers become meaningless
because they include every entry ever made.
SRS requirement: Dashboard defaults to current month.
A month selector lets admin view any past month's summary.
Fix: Add month/year state. Filter all data reads by date range before aggregating.
Farmers.jsx and Drivers.jsx use window.confirm() for delete confirmation.
This shows an English browser dialog, not a Tamil in-app dialog.
SRS requires: 'நீங்கள் நிச்சயமாக நீக்க விரும்புகிறீர்களா?' with styled confirm/cancel.
PWA Manifest App Name Not Tamil
When installed on Android, the home screen icon shows English instead of Tamil.
In DieselInput.jsx, the displayed total at the bottom uses:
Bug: 'value' in rupees mode is the raw input string (e.g. '2000'), not a number.
This could display '₹ 2000' correctly by coincidence but breaks when value is
undefined or empty. Should use: parseFloat(value || 0).toFixed(2) in both modes.
Also: the actual diesel.total is computed in DriverEntry.jsx via getDieselCost()
and stored correctly in the record. This is just a display inconsistency.
SelectField renderOption Bug — options with only ta: field
SelectField.jsx renders option labels as: opt.label || `${opt.en} (${opt.ta})`
Some option objects in the codebase have only {value, ta} (e.g., in Expenses.jsx
These render as 'undefined (இயந்திர பராமரிப்பு)' because opt.en is undefined.
Fix: Change the fallback to: opt.label || opt.en ? `${opt.en} (${opt.ta})` : opt.ta
The || operator means if durationHours is 0 (falsy), it shows '0.00' (string).
But if durationHours is 0.5, it shows 0.5 (number). Inconsistent display.
which consistently shows 2 decimal places at all times.
Add a simple role session to localStorage. No complex JWT or token system needed.
Replace the two-form system with the SRS-defined single entry form and net payable formula.
// On save — save to rl_work_entries (the SRS key):
// netPayable is NOT stored — computed on render
Replace the all-time accumulation with a per-day pending salary detection system.
// Detect days with driver logs that have NO salary entry yet
// Group logs by date for this driver
// Find dates that have no salary entry
// When admin confirms a day's salary:
payments: [], // partial payments added via addPayment()
// Corrected Dashboard — with month filter and correct expense aggregation
.filter(j => j.status !== 'cancelled' && inMonth(j.date || '')) // jobs need a date field!
// NOTE: jobs don't currently store a 'date' field — must be added on save
// (add: date: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0] when creating job)
// Expenses — harvester job level expenses (previously missing entirely)
otherExpenses: [] // already there, just needs UI
// Add this UI section in the Financials card:
throw new Error('Invalid backup: no schemaVersion field');
// Corrected Expenses.jsx form — add source field
// Add to form before save button:
Add to PricingConfig or new Settings page
Dashboard now filters by month before aggregating any totals.
Dashboard now includes harvester job expenses (totalExpense per job) in the expense total.
Worker expense now comes from rl_work_entries using (baseSalary + bonus + extra - advance) formula.
Driver salary expense comes from rl_driver_salary summing salaryEarned (not netPay).
Login sets role → role guards protect routes → Farmer sees only FarmerView.
Read all collections (filtered by selectedMonth + selectedYear):
├── workEntries (in month) ← was reading wrong key before!
└── expenses where source = 'own_farm' (in month) ← was mixing home expenses before!
Business Profit = Total Income - Total Business Expense
Home Expenses = sum(expenses where source = 'home_expense') — shown SEPARATELY
Lending = shown SEPARATELY, NOT included in profit
Follow this order strictly. Each fix unblocks the next one.
Do NOT attempt to fix multiple items at once.
Add date field to Harvester job on save
Fix Dashboard: month filter + correct aggregation
Fix vite.config.js PWA name to Tamil
Copy everything between the lines below and give it to the Anti-Gravity agent exactly as written.
SMARTUZHAVAN — CODE CORRECTION PROMPT FOR ANTI-GRAVITY AGENT
You are fixing and completing a React (Vite) project called SmartUzhavan.
The project is a Tamil-first, offline-first agricultural management system
for a rural entrepreneur in Tamil Nadu. It uses React 19 + Vite + LocalStorage.
No backend. No database. No Redux. No Tailwind.
Read the ENTIRE existing codebase before writing any code.
Read the SDLC documentation (RuralLedger_SDLC_v2.docx) before writing any code.
Do NOT redesign anything that already works correctly.
Do NOT introduce new libraries or patterns not already in the project.
WHAT WORKS CORRECTLY — DO NOT TOUCH THESE:
SessionEntry.jsx (except display fix), vite.config.js (except PWA name)
ISSUES TO FIX — IN THIS EXACT ORDER:
In SelectField.jsx, change the label fallback from:
FIX 4 — Workers.jsx — implement SRS net payable formula:
REPLACE the entire Workers.jsx with a single work entry form that has:
fields: date, workerId, workType, baseSalary, advance, bonus, extraAmount, notes
If netPayable < 0, show Tamil warning: 'முன்பணம் அதிகமாக கொடுக்கப்பட்டது'
REPLACE all-time hours accumulation with pending day detection.
Logic: Find dates that have driver logs but NO salary entry in rl_driver_salary.
Show each pending day as a card. Admin selects day → sees pre-filled hours and rate.
Admin confirms → save salary entry to rl_driver_salary.
Use PaymentHistory component for partial payment tracking on each salary entry.
Include source in every saved record.
FIX 7 — OwnFarmIncome.jsx — align to SRS schema:
Fields: harvestDate, cropType (text input), numberOfBags (number), pricePerBag (number)
Computed display (not a field): totalIncome = numberOfBags * pricePerBag
Store: { id, harvestDate, cropType, numberOfBags, pricePerBag, totalIncome, source:'own_farm' }
with Tamil labels: neighbour='அண்டை நபர்', normal='சாதாரண விவசாயி', own='சொந்த நிலம்'
a) Add date field: when saving a job, add date: new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0]
b) Add otherExpenses UI: a dynamic list of {category, amount} rows.
Categories: worker (தொழிலாளர்), mechanic (மெக்கானிக்), parts (உதிரி பாகங்கள்), food (உணவு)
FIX 10 — Dashboard.jsx — month filter and corrected aggregation:
Add month selector UI (dropdown for month + year)
Filter ALL data reads by selected month before aggregating
Expense = harvesterJobs.totalExpense + workEntries netPayable + driverSalary.salaryEarned + expenses(source=own_farm)
Show Home Expenses (expenses where source=home_expense) as a SEPARATE panel
Add: Pending Farmer Balance (sum all balances > 0 across harvester + rental)
Add: Driver Salary Unpaid (sum of (salaryEarned - sumPayments) per driver)
Change total display to use parseFloat(value||0).toFixed(2) for both modes
export getSession() — returns {role, userId, name} or null
Three login modes on one screen:
Admin: password input (password stored in localStorage as rl_admin_password;
if not set, first entry sets it)
Driver: select driver from list + enter 4-digit PIN (check against driver.pin)
Farmer: select farmer from list (no password needed)
On success: call setSession(role, id, name) then navigate to appropriate home page
Tamil labels on all fields. Large buttons.
Shows message + 'நிச்சயமாக நீக்க விரும்புகிறீர்களா?' in Tamil
Replace all window.confirm() calls in Farmers.jsx and Drivers.jsx
On mount: load session, get farmerId from session.userId
Show: harvester bills for this farmer (billId, season, hours, finalAmount, status)
Show: payment history per bill (reuse PaymentHistory in read-only mode)
Show: rental entries for this farmer
No edit/delete buttons. Tamil labels throughout.
Two sections: Lending and Home Expenses
LENDING: form fields: personName, village, amountGiven, dateLent, notes
Show list of lending records with repayments[] using PaymentHistory component
repayments[] uses same pattern as payments[]
Status: auto-derived (pending|partial|returned) from amountGiven vs sumPayments
amount, description. source ALWAYS = 'home_expense'. Save to rl_expenses.
exportData() — generates JSON file with schemaVersion:'2.0.0' and all 11 collections
importData(jsonString) — validates schemaVersion, restores all collections
Add Export/Import buttons to PricingConfig.jsx or create a Settings page
Tab or section switcher for 5 report types:
1. Monthly Summary: month/year filter → income by source, expense by source, profit
2. Machine-wise: season+year filter → group harvester jobs by machineType (tyre/track)
show: total hours, total revenue, total expense, net profit per machine
3. Farmer Balance: list all farmers with outstanding balance > 0
show: farmer name, village, bill IDs, total billed, total paid, balance
4. Driver Salary: driver + month filter → list days: hours worked, earned, paid, balance
5. Season Harvest: season+year filter → all jobs with Bill ID, farmer, hours, amount, status
After Login is built, update App.jsx routes:
/salary-driver → admin only (driver can VIEW own records only)
/ → redirect to /login if no session, else /dashboard
Navbar: Show only routes appropriate for the current role.
Add a 'Logout' button that calls clearSession() and redirects to /login.
1. Build and test ONE fix at a time. Confirm it works before moving to the next.
2. Do NOT rewrite files that are working — only modify the specific parts listed above.
3. Maintain the existing CSS module pattern (one .module.css per component).
4. Every new input field must have both English and Tamil labels using TamilLabel/InputField.
5. Every new success message must be in Tamil.
6. Do NOT use window.alert() or window.confirm() — use inline validation and ConfirmDialog.
7. Computed values (netPayable, amountPaid, balance, paymentStatus, totalIncome)
must NEVER be stored in localStorage — always computed on render.
8. Do NOT add new npm packages without explicit instruction.
9. When you complete a fix, list exactly what you changed and in which file.
10. If you are unsure about any requirement, refer to RuralLedger_SDLC_v2.docx.
If still unclear, ASK — do not guess or invent logic.
